purpose: Defines the `!logs [dashboardId] {filter}` command for displaying log entries from logs.json in the terminal. Supports filters: --level (error/warn/info/debug/permission/deviation), --task {id}, --agent {name}, --last {N}, --since {HH:MM}. Filters can be combined. Renders a 5-column table (Time/Task/Agent/Level/Message) and a summary line counting entries by level. Read-only.
gotchas: Custom log levels: `permission` triggers an amber popup on the dashboard alerting the user to check the terminal; `deviation` shows with a yellow badge in the log panel — both are non-standard log levels
--since uses HH:MM format and filters to entries after that time TODAY
Filters are applied in sequence — --last is applied AFTER other filters
Argument disambiguation: dashboardId (non-flag, non-task-id) vs filters (start with --) — task IDs contain dots
patterns: 6-step procedure: parse → read logs.json → apply filters in sequence → render table → handle empty → summary line
conventions: Syntax block lists every filter variant explicitly
Log Levels reference table at the bottom documenting all 6 level values and their dashboard rendering behavior
